jokes.text: Mopy Dick, that's what he is. - Moby Dick. - No, Mopy Dick.
jokes.setup: Leon tries to make a literary reference to describe Larry's depression
jokes.punchline: Leon confidently defends his malapropism 'Mopy Dick' against Jeff's correction
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Leon's confident ignorance creates great comedy through wordplay

jokes.text: Who's fucking Moby Dick? His dick is moping. Oh, literally Mopy Dick? Yeah, Mopy Dick.
jokes.setup: Jeff continues trying to correct Leon's literary reference
jokes.punchline: Leon escalates by literally interpreting his own malapropism - Larry's dick is actually moping
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation where Leon doubles down on his mistake with literal interpretation

jokes.text: Your brain is all fucking discombobulated, man.
jokes.setup: Leon offers relationship advice to depressed Larry
jokes.punchline: Leon uses the perfectly Leon word 'discombobulated' in earnest advice
jokes.explanation: Classic Leon vocabulary - discombobulated is perfectly in character

jokes.text: Larry, grow a mustache, man. Grow a goatee, a mustache, grow sideburns. Become another motherfucker, man.
jokes.setup: Leon gives Larry dating advice about getting out of his comfort zone
jokes.punchline: Leon's solution is hilariously literal - just grow facial hair to become a different person
jokes.explanation: Leon's earnest but absurd advice shows his unique problem-solving approach

jokes.text: What was that? What are you doing? You've got bedbugs? What's going on?
jokes.setup: Larry is clearly uncomfortable in bed, shifting around
jokes.punchline: Jeff notices Larry's squirming and assumes it's bedbugs rather than anal issues
jokes.explanation: Sets up Larry's discomfort that will pay off later - Jeff's obliviousness is funny

jokes.text: Eh, let's not go crazy.
jokes.setup: The family says Larry can always visit them after they move out
jokes.punchline: Larry immediately deflates their sentiment with typical blunt honesty
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry moment - deflating social niceties with uncomfortable honesty

jokes.text: Chill out? There's a gerbil running amok in my house. Come on, help me find it.
jokes.setup: Leon tells Larry to chill out about the missing gerbil
jokes.punchline: Larry's escalation - 'running amok' makes the gerbil sound like a dangerous threat
jokes.explanation: Sets up the gerbil plot while showing Larry's tendency to catastrophize small problems

jokes.text: Oh, Larry. Larry David, you are a romantic.
jokes.setup: Larry says he could get married again in a month
jokes.punchline: Antoinette calls Larry 'romantic' for his transactional view of love
jokes.explanation: Gentle irony about Larry's unromantic approach to romance

jokes.text: He was terrible, though. He was a terrible director.
jokes.setup: Larry mentions Matt Tessler directed four Seinfelds
jokes.punchline: Larry immediately undercuts this with brutal honesty about Matt's incompetence
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry - saying what everyone's thinking but shouldn't say

jokes.text: Well, I did the cancer scare episode of 'Home Improvement,' so I got typed as this guy who does ticker.
jokes.setup: Matt explains his career trajectory
jokes.punchline: He's been typecast as the director who handles medical/death storylines
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about Hollywood typecasting for morbid episodes

jokes.text: I want to get back to the funny, to the shtoomy.
jokes.setup: Matt wants to escape the drama ghetto
jokes.punchline: He uses the made-up Yiddish-sounding word 'shtoomy' for comedy
jokes.explanation: Invented word that sounds authentically Yiddish for comedy - well-crafted

jokes.text: Hey, Larry, do you still have that tickle in your anus?
jokes.setup: Matt is having a normal conversation with Larry
jokes.punchline: He casually brings up Larry's private medical issue loudly in public
jokes.explanation: Sets up the escalating embarrassment - Matt's casual mention is perfectly timed

jokes.text: Tickle in my-- what is she talking about? What are you talking about?
jokes.setup: Antoinette asks about Larry's discomfort
jokes.punchline: Larry's mortified confusion as his private issue becomes public
jokes.explanation: Larry's embarrassment builds the cringe factor effectively

jokes.text: Why did you say I have a tickle in my anus? Are you fucking crazy, huh? A tickle in my anus?
jokes.setup: Larry confronts Matt about revealing his private medical issue
jokes.punchline: Larry's outraged repetition only makes the situation more public and embarrassing
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe escalation - Larry makes it worse by protesting loudly

jokes.text: Larry's anus is fine. - Shut the fuck up, okay?
jokes.setup: Tessler tries to help by defending Larry's anus
jokes.punchline: Larry's violent rejection of the 'help'
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe escalation - the attempt to help makes everything infinitely worse

jokes.text: Hey, Richard, it's Larry. Matt Tessler just asked me to recommend him for your pilot... so this is me calling to recommend-- 'recommend' him.
jokes.setup: Larry leaves a voicemail recommendation for Matt
jokes.punchline: Larry awkwardly tries to convey air quotes in a voicemail by saying 'recommend'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-comedy about the impossibility of air quotes in voicemail - sets up major plot point

jokes.text: We could just put a sign up on the wall saying, 'Larry David was here.' Why don't we do that?
jokes.setup: Larry complains about the privacy issues with sign-in sheets
jokes.punchline: Larry escalates to absurd extreme - might as well advertise his presence
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Larry's paranoia about privacy taken to logical extreme

jokes.text: I'm not an inventor, but I'm an improver. I improve things that are broken. This is broken. This system is broken.
jokes.setup: Larry explains his mission to fix the sign-in sheet system
jokes.punchline: Larry's grandiose self-description as an 'improver' for trivial complaints
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry character moment - turning minor annoyance into grand mission

jokes.text: But I'm not a Jew, if that's what you're thinking. 'Cause I saw your wheels spinning.
jokes.setup: Paula mentions she's from New York
jokes.punchline: Paula assumes Larry is stereotyping and calls him out awkwardly
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable moment that establishes Paula as someone who makes assumptions

jokes.text: That's uncanny. It's like there's a window into my soul and you can just really read me.
jokes.setup: Paula claims she can read Larry's thoughts about Jewish stereotypes
jokes.punchline: Larry's sarcastic response treats her presumption as mystical ability
jokes.explanation: Larry's perfect sarcastic deflection of Paula's awkward assumption

jokes.text: did you ever go out with a bald guy? Yeah. How did you find it? It was magical.
jokes.setup: Larry asks about Paula's experience dating bald men
jokes.punchline: Paula's deadpan 'magical' response to Larry's fishing for compliments
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff with Paula playing along with Larry's obvious fishing

jokes.text: How am I doing on the flirt so far? How would you assess it? Um... Pretty good? I'm-- well, 7.5.
jokes.setup: Larry asks for a numerical rating of his flirting performance
jokes.punchline: Paula gives him a specific score like he's being judged in competition
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about dating performance anxiety - Larry's need for validation is funny

jokes.text: I'm going to New York. Why am I stopping off in Florida?
jokes.setup: Nurse insists Larry tell her his problem before seeing doctor
jokes.punchline: Larry's analogy comparing medical bureaucracy to inefficient travel
jokes.explanation: Excellent analogy that perfectly captures medical bureaucracy inefficiency

jokes.text: Okay, I have a gerbil up my ass. That's my problem.
jokes.setup: Larry refuses to tell the nurse his real problem
jokes.punchline: Larry gives an outrageous fake diagnosis to end the conversation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry moment - choosing outrageous lie over minor inconvenience

jokes.text: How long has it been there? Two days.
jokes.setup: Nurse takes Larry's gerbil claim seriously and asks follow-up
jokes.punchline: Larry commits to the lie with specific timeline
jokes.explanation: Good escalation as Larry has to maintain his absurd lie

jokes.text: You're listening to me? I don't know what I'm talking about. Put the sign-in sheet back on.
jokes.setup: Receptionist changed policy based on Larry's complaint
jokes.punchline: Larry admits he doesn't know what he's talking about when his complaint is taken seriously
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry - complains about system then wants old system back when changed

jokes.text: How much will you give me to start gargling now? 50 bucks. Just do it.
jokes.setup: Larry proposes random bet about gargling
jokes.punchline: Jeff immediately agrees to pay Larry to gargle for entertainment
jokes.explanation: Absurd moment showing Jeff's willingness to pay for Larry's antics

jokes.text: I just got 50 bucks. No, now that doesn't count. It was so enjoyable and you ruined it with 'I just got 50 bucks.'
jokes.setup: Larry celebrates getting the money
jokes.punchline: Jeff retroactively cancels the enjoyment because Larry gloated about the money
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Jeff's specific rules for his own entertainment

jokes.text: Do you know who anyone is? No. When was the last time you bought an album? 1972?
jokes.setup: Larry doesn't know current R&B singer John Legend
jokes.punchline: Larry admits he hasn't bought an album since 1972, explaining his ignorance
jokes.explanation: Good character work establishing Larry's complete disconnection from pop culture

jokes.text: What do I do? Am I allowed to hold hands during the mo--? Is there a rule?
jokes.setup: Larry asks about dating etiquette since he's been out of practice
jokes.punchline: Larry treats dating like there are official rules he needs to learn
jokes.explanation: Shows Larry's social awkwardness treating romance like a rule book

jokes.text: That's the guy who walked past the line to get his ticket before. Pete! Look at him. Oh my God. He was faking. Are you kidding me? No. That's really good. I gotta take my hat off to him.
jokes.setup: They see someone who cut in line earlier, now walking normally
jokes.punchline: Larry admires the line-cutter's fake disability scam despite being the victim
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry moment - admiring the audacity of someone who wronged him

jokes.text: It's just you're squirming a lot. You're squirming more than I am. Well, my seat is uncomfortable. Yeah, my seat's uncomfortable too.
jokes.setup: Paula notices Larry squirming during the movie
jokes.punchline: They're both squirming but Larry can't admit his real medical reason
jokes.explanation: Good visual callback to Larry's discomfort while maintaining his cover story

jokes.text: I've never heard of anybody going to a gastroenterologist for a checkup. Oh, really? That's what you were doing there. Is that what I said? Yes, that's what you said.
jokes.setup: Paula questions Larry's story about the gastroenterologist visit
jokes.punchline: Larry's caught in his lie but can't admit the real reason without embarrassment
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Larry's deception getting harder to maintain

jokes.text: I have friends who I can't stand, you know, for, like, 25 years. I'm still friends with them. Why? Because I stay to the end, till they're dead.
jokes.setup: Larry explains his philosophy about finishing things he starts
jokes.punchline: Larry reveals he maintains friendships he hates until people literally die
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark character insight - Larry's pathological need to finish things taken to morbid extreme

jokes.text: I'm not even the captain, I'm going down with the ship. That's how I want you to think about me: I'm the guy who keeps the captain company.
jokes.setup: Larry continues his 'going down with the ship' metaphor
jokes.punchline: Larry positions himself as even less important than the captain but equally doomed
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry self-description - martyring himself for the least important reasons

jokes.text: You don't understand women. You don't understand etiquette. Fine.
jokes.setup: Jeff and Larry argue about whether Paula will wait
jokes.punchline: Larry's resigned 'Fine' when his etiquette principles conflict with reality
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Larry choosing principle over pragmatism

jokes.text: Yeah, well, she has a tickle in her anus anyway. She has a tickle in her anus? I think so. She was squirming in her seat.
jokes.setup: Larry dismisses Paula after she left
jokes.punchline: Larry projects his own medical issue onto Paula based on her squirming
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Larry's assumption everyone shares his problems

jokes.text: Then there's a hole in the nose. We'll just cover it with makeup. Blech!
jokes.setup: Jeff suggests his neighbor can remove her nose rings for the date
jokes.punchline: Larry's disgusted 'Blech!' at the mental image of nose holes covered with makeup
jokes.explanation: Larry's visceral reaction to the makeshift solution is appropriately gross

jokes.text: We'll go to lunch together. Every day, every day. Every day. Every day, lunch.
jokes.setup: The neighbor wants to be friends
jokes.punchline: The neighbor's escalating insistence on daily lunch dates horrifies Larry
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the neighbor's neediness that drives Larry away

jokes.text: No no no, it was a test to see if he's a tolerant person. And anyone who's not is no neighbor of mine.
jokes.setup: Matt thinks Larry was making fun of disabled people
jokes.punchline: Larry's post-hoc rationalization that his offensive act was actually a tolerance test
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry rationalization - turning his inappropriate behavior into virtue

jokes.text: My son has an office on the right hand of Jesus.
jokes.setup: Larry offers to rent an office to Matt's disabled son
jokes.punchline: Matt's declaration that his son is dead, delivered as a dramatic put-down
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny revelation that elevates the emotional stakes of Matt's anger

jokes.text: Hey, you know what's really funny? A successful writer-producer who puts gerbils up his ass for fun.
jokes.setup: Matt is angry about Larry's behavior
jokes.punchline: Matt escalates by spreading the gerbil rumor in retaliation
jokes.explanation: Important plot escalation where Larry's lie becomes public scandal

jokes.text: Why'd you 'recommend' him, Mr. Quotehead?
jokes.setup: Larry explains his air quotes strategy
jokes.punchline: Richard's angry 'Mr. Quotehead' nickname for Larry's failed communication
jokes.explanation: Great insult that perfectly captures Richard's frustration with Larry's communication style

jokes.text: You call him Ben Laden or Bin Laden? I don't know. You called him Ben. That's almost like a Jewish name. That's true. Ben Laden does sound like a shirt-maker in Manhattan.
jokes.setup: They argue about pronouncing Bin Laden's name
jokes.punchline: They realize 'Ben Laden' sounds like a Jewish businessman in the garment district
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay observation about how pronunciation changes cultural context

jokes.text: You know, I do have a tickle in my anus. Oh, really? Yeah. I gotta split.
jokes.setup: Larry finally admits his real medical issue
jokes.punchline: Richard's immediate departure after this TMI revelation
jokes.explanation: Good payoff where Larry's honesty drives people away as much as his lies

jokes.text: The gerbil got on the elevator, hit 'up' and went up your asshole? Is that what happened?
jokes.setup: Leon confronts Larry about the gerbil rumors
jokes.punchline: Leon's elaborate scenario of the gerbil taking the elevator to Larry's anus
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd Leon logic connecting the missing gerbil to the rumors

jokes.text: there's a guest here spreading a vicious, nasty, scurrilous rumor about me and a gerbil. Okay? I'm sure you've heard it. And there's not a word of truth to it, okay?
jokes.setup: Larry is giving a toast at the bat mitzvah
jokes.punchline: Larry uses a celebratory speech to address his personal scandal
jokes.explanation: Peak Larry moment - hijacking a child's celebration for personal vindication

jokes.text: However, in the interest of full disclosure I will tell you that, um, I do have a tickle in my anus. And they're slightly related to each other.
jokes.setup: Larry denies the gerbil rumor at the bat mitzvah
jokes.punchline: Larry admits his real anal problem, connecting it to the gerbil rumors
jokes.explanation: Masterful escalation where Larry's honesty is even more inappropriate than the lies

jokes.text: What are you looking at, Tessler? I'll put a fist up your ass. How about that, huh?
jokes.setup: Larry finishes his inappropriate bat mitzvah speech
jokes.punchline: Larry threatens Matt with anal violence, continuing the anal theme
jokes.explanation: Good escalation where Larry's threat ironically reinforces the anal fixation

jokes.text: My underwear? You're in the no-fly zone? I'm going right over the fence. You're over the fence? Over the fence. Did you miss the gate? Not at all. Don't even think about the gate?
jokes.setup: Larry and Cheryl discuss her underwear choice
jokes.punchline: Extended metaphor about underwear styles using airport/fence imagery
jokes.explanation: Clever extended metaphor that maintains the sexual tension between exes

jokes.text: Oh, fuck you, Larry. Bullshit, motherfucker. I didn't know. You are such a bald asshole! Who the fuck you think you're talking to? Don't nobody talk to my man like that. You'd better get your ass out of my house, you fucking bitch.
jokes.setup: Larry inadvertently causes problems with Leon's soccer game
jokes.punchline: Explosive confrontation with Loretta defending Leon and kicking Larry out
jokes.explanation: Explosive finale showing the family dynamic and Larry's final ejection from the house
